searchDocs.title: Markdown​
searchDocs.content: Syntax	Result**bold**	bold *italic*	italic __underline__	underline ~~strike~~	strike `code`	inline code ||spoiler||	click-to-reveal spoiler {red|text} / {#ff0000|text}	coloured text (named or hex) [label](url)	link — fires the LinkClicked event > quoted	blockquote # Title	heading (#, ##, ###) :fire:	emoji @Name	mention  Each feature can be toggled globally under parsing:  Bold.Configure({ parsing = { markdown = true, mentions = true, emoji = true, links = true, panels = true }, })

searchDocs.title: Mentions​
searchDocs.content: @Name (or @DisplayName) resolves to a player on the server, against the filtered text, so a masked name can't ping anyone. When the local player is named, their card highlights and flashes, and Bold.OnMentioned fires.  Bold.OnMentioned(function(message) SoundService:PlayLocalSound(pingSound) end)

searchDocs.title: Emoji & stickers​
searchDocs.content: Built-in :name: emoji are ready to go. Register your own:  Bold.RegisterEmoji("plinko", "rbxassetid://123") Bold.RegisterSticker("gg", "rbxassetid://456")   Typing @, : or / in the input box opens an autocomplete popup (players, emoji + stickers, and commands respectively).

searchDocs.title: Custom panels​
searchDocs.content: A :::name … ::: fence renders a component you register. Arguments are parsed as key="value" pairs (and bare flags); everything between the fences is the panel body.  Bold.RegisterPanel("poll", function(args, message, theme) local frame = Instance.new("Frame") frame.AutomaticSize = Enum.AutomaticSize.Y frame.Size = UDim2.new(1, 0, 0, 0) -- build from args.question and args.options ("A,B,C") return frame end)   A message containing:  :::poll question="Best map?" options="A,B,C" vote below :::   renders your poll frame inline, with args = { question = "Best map?", options = "A,B,C", _positional = {} } and args's body available as panel_body. Unregistered panel names render a graceful placeholder. Bold ships :::divider::: and :::note::: as examples.

searchDocs.title: Color​
searchDocs.content: </> Builder:Color( color: Color3 | string, s: string ) → () Appends coloured text. color is a Color3 or a name/hex string ("red", "#ff0000").

searchDocs.title: Link​
searchDocs.content: </> Builder:Link( label: string, url: string ) → () Appends a clickable link (fires the LinkClicked event when tapped).

searchDocs.title: Mention​
searchDocs.content: </> Builder:Mention(playerOrName: Player | string) → () Appends an @mention of a player (or a raw name).

searchDocs.title: Panel​
searchDocs.content: </> Builder:Panel( name: string, args: {[string]: any}?, body: string? ) → () Appends a :::name … ::: panel fence for a renderer registered with Bold.RegisterPanel. args become the panel's key/value arguments.

searchDocs.title: Embed​
searchDocs.content: </> Builder:Embed(embed: Types.Embed) → () Attaches an embed (from Bold.Image, Bold.Action, etc.).

searchDocs.title: Kind​
searchDocs.content: </> Builder:Kind(kind: string) → () Sets the message kind (chat, system, announcement, whisper, team, or your own).

searchDocs.title: Reply​
searchDocs.content: </> Builder:Reply(message: any) → () Marks this message as a reply to another, showing a quoted preview.

searchDocs.title: To​
searchDocs.content: </> Builder:To(player: Player ) → () Whispers the message to a single player (delivered to them and echoed to the sender).

searchDocs.title: Payload​
searchDocs.content: </> Builder:Payload() → any Returns the assembled { text, embeds, kind, tags, reply_to, recipient_id, pinned } payload without sending it.

searchDocs.title: Send​
searchDocs.content: </> Builder:Send() → () Sends the assembled message through Bold's pipeline.

searchDocs.title: Theme​
searchDocs.content: The theme is a flat table of colour/typography tokens (background, text, accent, mention_bg, code_text, spoiler_bg, font, corner_radius, …). Merge overrides, or apply a preset.  Bold.Theme("midnight") -- "dark" | "light" | "midnight" | "neon" Bold.SetTheme({ accent = Color3.fromRGB(0, 200, 160), mention_bg = Color3.fromRGB(0, 200, 160), font = Enum.Font.Gotham, })

searchDocs.title: Layout​
searchDocs.content: Bold.Layout("bottom_left") -- top_left | top_right | bottom_left | bottom_right | center Bold.Configure({ interface = { density = "cozy", -- "compact" | "cozy" | "comfortable" window_size = UDim2.fromOffset(460, 0), max_list_height = 400, max_messages = 200, show_timestamps = true, message_grouping = true, draggable = true, }, })

searchDocs.title: Visuals & social​
searchDocs.content: Bold.Configure({ interface = { backdrop = true, -- the panel behind the whole list message_background = true, -- a card behind each message (Discord-ish) collapsible = true, -- long messages get a Show more/less toggle collapse_lines = 6, open_animation = true, -- animate the window opening/closing draggable = true, -- drag by the header/grip resizable = true, -- resize from the bottom-right grip show_friend_badge = true, -- a dot next to friends' names }, })   A single message can carry its own card colour:  Bold.Message({ content = { raw_text = "VIP joined!" }, background = Color3.fromRGB(120, 80, 220), background_transparency = 0.7, })   Players hide others locally with /ignore <player> (and /unignore); ignored users' messages simply don't render for them.

searchDocs.title: Keybinds​
searchDocs.content: Bold drives its keybinds through Switch when present, falling back to UserInputService. Every bind is rebindable, or disable one with false.  Bold.Configure({ keys = { focus = Enum.KeyCode.Slash, -- focus the input box toggle = Enum.KeyCode.F1, -- show/hide the window up = { Enum.KeyCode.Up }, down = { Enum.KeyCode.Down }, accept = { Enum.KeyCode.Return, Enum.KeyCode.Tab }, close = { Enum.KeyCode.Escape }, }, })

searchDocs.title: Animation​
searchDocs.content: Bold.Configure({ animation = { enabled = true, in_style = "slide", -- "slide" | "fade" | "none" in_time = 0.18, stagger = 0.02, smooth_scroll = true, }, })

searchDocs.title: Events & hooks​
searchDocs.content: Observe the whole lifecycle with Bold.On(name, fn) — MessageSent, MessageReceived, MessageRendered, MessageUpdated, Mentioned, ReactionAdded, ReplyClicked, LinkClicked, CardClicked, FilterApplied, ThemeChanged, and more. For inline decoration, append rich text to every row:  Bold.Decorate(function(message) if message.metadata.author_id and isVIP(message.metadata.author_id) then return "<font color=\"rgb(255,210,80)\">★</font>" end end)   See the Bold API for the full surface.

searchDocs.title: Install​
searchDocs.content: Drop the Bold module into ReplicatedStorage, via any of:  HTTP bootstrap — paste into the Studio command bar (needs Game Settings → Security → Allow HTTP Requests): loadstring(game:GetService("HttpService"):GetAsync("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mkl48/BOLD/main/dist/bootstrap.luau"))() Wally — Bold = "kr3ative/bold@0.1.0" Rojo — sync the src tree in as a ModuleScript named Bold.

searchDocs.title: Start it​
searchDocs.content: Bold is realm-split. Start it on the server so it can receive, filter and broadcast, and on the client so it builds the UI.  -- server local Bold = require(game.ReplicatedStorage.Bold) Bold.Start()   -- client local Bold = require(game.ReplicatedStorage.Bold) Bold.Start({ interface = { channel_name = "Global", window_position = UDim2.new(0, 12, 1, -12), window_anchor = Vector2.new(0, 1), }, })   That's the whole loop. The input box appears, players type, and messages flow. Press / to focus the box (rebindable).

searchDocs.title: Send from code​
searchDocs.content: -- quick path Bold.Message({ content = { raw_text = "Welcome **home** :wave:" } }) -- fluent builder Bold.Compose() :Text("gg ") :Mention(somePlayer) :Emoji("fire") :Send()   On the server, Bold.Message/Bold.Compose():Send() broadcast (or whisper, with :To(player)). On the client they send through the same pipeline as the input box.

searchDocs.title: React to events​
searchDocs.content: Bold.OnMentioned(function(message) -- the local player was @mentioned end) Bold.On("MessageReceived", function(message) print(message.metadata.author_name, message.content.raw_text) end)   Next: Sending messages, Formatting & panels, Moderation, and Customization.

searchDocs.title: What you get​
searchDocs.content: Markdown — **bold**, *italic*, __underline__, ~~strike~~, `code`, ||spoiler||, {color|text}, [links](url), > quotes, and :emoji:.Real mentions — @Name resolves to a player server-side, highlights in the message, flashes the mentioned player's card, and fires Bold.OnMentioned.Custom panels — a :::name … ::: fence renders a component you register with Bold.RegisterPanel.Reactions & replies — hover a message to react or quote-reply.Slash commands — register /commands; ships /me, /shrug, /clear, /help and /incog private whispers.Deep customization — one Bold.Configure deep-merge over theme, density, layout, parsing, filtering, keybinds, animation and sounds.

searchDocs.title: The pipeline​
searchDocs.content: compose (client): raw text + embeds → local hard-block guard → server: stamp → fast filter (TextService + hard-block, cached) → deliver instantly → PurgoMalum (deferred, non-blocking) → patch in place if it catches more → client: hard-block backstop → parse filtered text → render   Delivery only ever waits on the fast, cached layers, so a slow third-party service can never stall chat.  Continue to Getting started.

searchDocs.title: The pipeline​
searchDocs.content: fast (blocking, authoritative, cached): Roblox TextService → local Advise backstop → sets the delivered text; everything renders from this deferred (non-blocking, best-effort): PurgoMalum → runs AFTER the message is on screen; patches it in place if it catches more (via an UPDATE channel) client backstop: Advise runs again at render — "filter on our side too"   Because delivery only ever waits on the fast, cached layers, a slow or down third-party service can never stall chat. The deferred pass edits the already-visible message a beat later if needed.  Crucially, the client sends raw text and parses the filtered text at render time — the filter always governs what is shown.

searchDocs.title: Configuring it​
searchDocs.content: Bold.Configure({ filter = { enabled = true, -- master switch purgomalum = true, -- run the deferred third-party pass client_backstop = true, -- re-run the local hard-block at render }, })   purgomalum uses purgomalum.com and is fail-open — any error or timeout leaves the text unchanged. It needs Allow HTTP Requests enabled; turn it off if you don't want the dependency.

searchDocs.title: Advise & the word list​
searchDocs.content: Advise is the local backstop layered on top of TextService (not a replacement). It normalizes text before matching — collapsing spacing/punctuation, mapping leetspeak, and treating vowels as optional — so f u c k, f.u.c.k, f0ck and even vowel-dropped fck are caught, with masking projected back onto the original text. An allow-list handles Scunthorpe-style exceptions.  Its blocklist (a few thousand words) is sourced from Proflis as JSON data, not as string literals in any script — so Roblox's automated moderation never flags Bold's own source for the words it filters. The server fetches the list once over HttpService and relays it to clients through a RemoteFunction.  Enable HttpService for the full experience Proflis fetches the word list over HttpService:GetAsync. If HTTP is disabled the local list is simply empty and Bold falls back to Roblox's TextService filter alone — everything still works, just with a smaller backstop. Turn on Game Settings → Security → Allow HTTP Requests to get the full local filter (and the same is required for the PurgoMalum layer).  Extend or refine it at runtime:  Bold.AddBlockedWords({ "myword", "anotherone" }) Bold.AddAllowedWords({ "assassin", "class" }) -- never mask these   Keep additions narrow: matching is substring-based, so it's only safe for words that never legitimately appear inside other words. TextService remains the authoritative layer.

searchDocs.title: Dropping messages​
searchDocs.content: For custom rules, install a guard. Returning false drops the message before delivery.  -- server Bold.Guard(function(message, player) if isMuted(player) then return false end return true end)   The live input box also shows a warning pill the moment a locally-known blocked word is typed, before the player even sends.

searchDocs.title: Formatting your messages​
searchDocs.content: Wrap text in these to style it:  Type this	You get**bold**	bold *italic*	italic __underline__	underline ~~strike~~	strikethrough `code`	inline code ||secret||	a spoiler — click to reveal {red|hi} or {#ff8800|hi}	coloured text [click](https://…)	a link > quoted	a quote block # Title	a big heading (#, ##, ###)

searchDocs.title: Mentioning people​
searchDocs.content: Type @ and start typing a name — a list pops up, pick with the arrow keys and Tab/Enter. Mentioning someone highlights the message for them and pings them.  nice shot @Builderman!

searchDocs.title: Emoji & stickers​
searchDocs.content: Type : then a name (:fire:, :tada:) and pick from the list. Stickers show up in the same menu.

searchDocs.title: Spoilers​
searchDocs.content: Hide something with ||…||. It shows as a covered block until someone clicks the message to reveal it.  the boss is ||actually a robot||

searchDocs.title: Reactions & replies​
searchDocs.content: Hover a message to get its toolbar:  ☺ — react with an emoji.↩ — reply. Your message shows a little quote of theirs.

searchDocs.title: Commands​
searchDocs.content: Start a message with /:  Command	What it does/me <action>	* You wave /shrug	adds ¯\_(ツ)_/¯ /poll Question | A | B	starts a poll with clickable options /incog <player> <message>	private whisper to one player /ignore <player>	hide someone's messages for you /unignore <player>	stop ignoring them /clear	clear your own chat /help	list every command  For /incog and /ignore, start typing a name after the command and pick from the popup.

searchDocs.title: Polls​
searchDocs.content: /poll builds a little poll card with clickable options:  /poll Best map? | Ruins | Bunker | Rooftop   Everyone sees the poll and can tap an option.    Long messages collapse with a Show more link, and older messages fade — hover them to bring them back.

searchDocs.title: The quick path​
searchDocs.content: Bold.Message(config) constructs and routes a message. On the server it broadcasts; on the client it goes through the send pipeline.  Bold.Message({ content = { raw_text = "server announcement", }, kind = "announcement", })

searchDocs.title: The builder​
searchDocs.content: Bold.Compose() returns a fluent builder that assembles the text (in Bold's markdown) plus structured embeds, then :Send()s it.  Bold.Compose() :Bold("Round over!") :Line() :Text("winner: ") :Mention(winner) :Space() :Color(Color3.fromRGB(255, 210, 80), "+50") :Kind("announcement") :Send()   Chainable inline builders: :Text :Bold :Italic :Underline :Strike :Code :Spoiler :Color :Link :Mention :Emoji :Quote :Space :Line. Structure & metadata: :Panel :Embed :Image :Sticker :Kind :Tag :Reply :To :Pin. Terminals: :Send() and :Payload().

searchDocs.title: Embeds​
searchDocs.content: Bold.Message({ content = { raw_text = "new skin dropped", embeds = { Bold.Image("rbxassetid://123", { caption = "Midnight" }), Bold.Action("buy_skin", { title = "500 coins", button_text = "Buy", style = "primary" }), }, }, })   Embed constructors: Bold.Image, Bold.Gif, Bold.Sticker, Bold.RichText, Bold.Action, Bold.Buttons (a row of buttons), and Bold.Progress (a 0–1 bar). Listen for any button (and poll option) with:  Bold.OnAction(function(action_id, message) if action_id == "buy_skin" then ... end end)

searchDocs.title: Replies & reactions​
searchDocs.content: Players reply and react from the hover toolbar on each message. From code:  Bold.Compose():Text("agreed"):Reply(someMessage):Send() Bold.On("ReactionAdded", function(id, emoji, userId) ... end)   Configure the quick-reaction set and toggle either feature under interface:  Bold.Configure({ interface = { reactions = true, reply_enabled = true, quick_reactions = { "👍", "❤️", "😂", "🔥", "🎉" }, }, })

searchDocs.title: Whispers​
searchDocs.content: Set a recipient and the message is delivered only to them (and echoed back to the sender), filtered per-viewer:  Bold.Compose():Text("meet me at spawn"):To(targetPlayer):Send()   The built-in /incog <player> <message> command does exactly this from the input box.

searchDocs.title: Kinds​
searchDocs.content: Every message has a kind — chat, system, announcement, whisper, team, me, or your own. Kinds drive the row's prefix, colour and grouping. Restyle one, or register a fully custom renderer:  Bold.SetKindStyle("vip", "announcement_text", "★ ", true) Bold.RegisterRenderer("trade", function(message, theme) local frame = Instance.new("Frame") -- your bespoke card return frame end)

searchDocs.title: Configure​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Configure(config: Types.BoldConfig?) → () Deep-merges a [BoldConfig] into the live config. Safe before or after Bold.Start. Sections: interface, parsing, filter, keys, animation, sounds.

searchDocs.title: Theme​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Theme(preset: string) → boolean Applies a built-in theme preset (dark, light, midnight, neon). Returns false if the name is unknown.

searchDocs.title: Layout​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Layout(preset: string) → boolean Applies a window layout preset (top_left, top_right, bottom_left, bottom_right, center). Returns false if the name is unknown.

searchDocs.title: Start​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Start(config: Types.BoldConfig?) → () Boots Bold on the current realm. On the server it wires the receive → filter → broadcast pipeline; on the client it builds the UI and input. Accepts an optional [BoldConfig] (the same shape Bold.Configure takes). Call once per realm.

searchDocs.title: Message​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Message(config: Types.MessageClass?) → Types.MessageClass Constructs and sends a message. On the server it broadcasts, or whispers whenmetadata.recipient_id is set; on the client it goes through the send pipeline.

searchDocs.title: Compose​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Compose() → Builder Returns a fluent Builder for assembling a message from markdown + embeds, e.g. Bold.Compose():Bold("gg"):Mention(plr):Send().

searchDocs.title: Dispatch​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server </> Bold.Dispatch( config: Types.MessageClass?, target: Player? ) → Types.MessageClass? Sends a server-originated message without routing through the client SEND channel. Pass target to whisper it to one player.

searchDocs.title: Image​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Image( image_id: string, config: Types.ImageEmbed? ) → Types.ImageEmbed Builds an Image embed for a message's content.embeds.

searchDocs.title: Gif​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Gif( video_id: string, config: { loop: boolean?, autoplay: boolean?, color: Color3? }? ) → Types.GifEmbed Builds a Gif (VideoFrame) embed for a message's content.embeds.

searchDocs.title: Sticker​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Sticker( sticker_id: string, config: { size: number?, color: Color3? }? ) → Types.StickerEmbed Builds a Sticker embed for a message's content.embeds.

searchDocs.title: RichText​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.RichText(config: Types.RichTextEmbed) → Types.RichTextEmbed Builds a RichText embed (title, description, fields) for a message's content.embeds.

searchDocs.title: Action​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Action( action_id: string, config: { title: string?, button_text: string?, style: ("primary" | "danger" | "ghost")?, color: Color3? , }? ) → Types.ActionEmbed Builds an Action (button) embed. Handle clicks via the EmbedActionFired event.

searchDocs.title: Buttons​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Buttons( buttons: {Types.ButtonSpec}, config: { title: string?, color: Color3? }? ) → Types.ButtonsEmbed Builds a row-of-buttons embed. Handle clicks with Bold.OnAction.

searchDocs.title: Progress​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Progress( value: number, config: { label: string?, color: Color3? }? ) → Types.ProgressEmbed Builds a progress-bar embed. value is 0..1.

searchDocs.title: On​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.On( eventName: string, callback: (...any) → () ) → RBXScriptConnection  Connects to a Bold event by name (MessageReceived, Mentioned, ReactionAdded, LinkClicked, ThemeChanged, …). Returns the connection.

searchDocs.title: Once​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Once( eventName: string, callback: (...any) → () ) → RBXScriptConnection  Like Bold.On, but auto-disconnects after the first fire.

searchDocs.title: Hook​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Hook( hookName: string, fn: (...any) → () ) → () Registers an observational lifecycle hook (BeforeSend, AfterDeliver, BeforeRender, …). Fire-and-forget: it can observe but not alter or drop a message.

searchDocs.title: OnMentioned​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.OnMentioned(fn: (message: any) → ()) → RBXScriptConnection  Fires when the local player is @mentioned in a delivered message.

searchDocs.title: OnAction​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.OnAction(fn: ( action_id: string, message: any? ) → ()) → RBXScriptConnection  Fires when an Action/Buttons embed button (or a poll option) is clicked.action_id is the id you gave the button; poll options fire "poll:<option>".

searchDocs.title: Guard​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Guard(fn: ( message: any, player: Player? ) → (boolean?)) → () Installs a message guard for the current realm. Return false to drop a message before it is delivered (server) or rendered (client).

searchDocs.title: Classify​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Classify( message: Types.MessageClass, kind: string, tags: {string}? ) → Types.MessageClass Sets a message's kind (and optional tags), firing the relevant events.

searchDocs.title: Tag​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Tag( message: Types.MessageClass, ...: string ) → Types.MessageClass Adds one or more tags to a message, de-duplicated.

searchDocs.title: Pin​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Pin(message: Types.MessageClass) → Types.MessageClass Pins a message card (adds the pinned indicator).

searchDocs.title: Unpin​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.Unpin(message: Types.MessageClass) → Types.MessageClass Unpins a message card.

searchDocs.title: React​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.React( message: any, emoji: string ) → () Toggles the local player's reaction (an emoji) on a message. Accepts a message object or a message id.

searchDocs.title: RegisterEmoji​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.RegisterEmoji( name: string, assetId: string ) → () Registers a custom :name: emoji backed by an image asset id.

searchDocs.title: RegisterSticker​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.RegisterSticker( id: string, assetId: string ) → () Registers a sticker id → asset mapping used by sticker embeds and autocomplete.

searchDocs.title: SearchEmoji​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.SearchEmoji(query: string) → {string} Searches built-in + custom emoji by partial name.

searchDocs.title: RegisterCommand​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.RegisterCommand( name: string, spec: Types.CommandSpec ) → () Registers a slash /command. The handler returns a compose payload ({ text, kind?, recipient?, embeds? }) to send, or nothing for a local-only command. Built-ins: /me, /shrug, /clear, /help, /incog.

searchDocs.title: RegisterPanel​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.RegisterPanel( name: string, fn: ( args: any, message: any, theme: any ) → Frame ) → () Registers a renderer for a :::name … ::: panel fence. fn(args, message, theme) returns a Frame. Unregistered names render a graceful placeholder.

searchDocs.title: AddBlockedWords​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.AddBlockedWords(words: {string} | string) → () Adds words to the local [Advise] blocklist (layered under Roblox's TextService filter). The bulk list is loaded from Proflis; this is for one-off additions.

searchDocs.title: AddAllowedWords​
searchDocs.content: </> Bold.AddAllowedWords(words: {string} | string) → () Allow-lists words so a blocked substring inside them is never masked (Scunthorpe-style exceptions), e.g. Bold.AddAllowedWords("assassin").

searchDocs.title: SetTheme​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.SetTheme(theme: Types.Theme) → () Merges theme token overrides into the live theme (see also Bold.Theme presets).

searchDocs.title: SetDensity​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.SetDensity(d: "compact" | "cozy" | "comfortable") → () Sets the message row density.

searchDocs.title: FocusInput​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.FocusInput() → () Focuses the chat input box.

searchDocs.title: SetKindStyle​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.SetKindStyle( kind: string, textKey: string, prefix: string?, bold: boolean? ) → () Restyles a message kind's prefix, text-colour token and bold flag.

searchDocs.title: SetKindOverride​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.SetKindOverride( kind: string, bgKey: string, textKey: string ) → () Overrides the background/text theme keys used for a message kind's card.

searchDocs.title: Decorate​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.Decorate(fn: (any) → string?) → () Registers a per-row decorator that appends inline rich text; fn(message) returns a string (or nil to add nothing).

searchDocs.title: SetChannelName​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.SetChannelName(name: string) → () Sets the header/channel label.

searchDocs.title: Toggle​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.Toggle(visible: boolean?) → () Shows or hides the chat window (or forces a state).

searchDocs.title: Clear​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.Clear() → () Clears the local message list.

searchDocs.title: RegisterRenderer​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Bold.RegisterRenderer( kind: string, fn: ( any, any ) → Frame ) → () Registers a fully custom card renderer for a message kind; fn(message, theme) returns a Frame used instead of the default row.
